agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Well-instrumented with request IDs and structured logging"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse","status":"broken","issue":"Non-idempotent writes mean retries (from circuit breaker recovery, exponential backoff, or lost ACKs) produce duplicate records. This is not an edge case — it is the expected behavior of at-least-once delivery under normal operation."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ is a write-only black hole. No consumer, no alerting on queue depth, no reprocessing path. Failed events silently accumulate and are permanently lost in practice."},{"from":"retry mechanism","to":"data warehouse","status":"broken","issue":"Exponential backoff retries re-submit the same event payload without an idempotency key. Every retry is a guaranteed duplicate row in the warehouse."}],"contract_compliance":"The service promises event delivery but actually delivers event duplication. At-least-once semantics without idempotent writes is a data correctness bug, not a design tradeoff.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. Two independent paths produce duplicates: (1) normal retry after transient warehouse failure, (2) lost ACK causing upstream re-emission. No deduplication exists anywhere in the pipeline to compensate."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":[]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong — Prometheus histograms, p99 dashboards, alerting configured","gaps":["No metric tracking duplicate event rate or deduplication ratio","No metric on DLQ depth or DLQ age-of-oldest-message","No metric distinguishing first-attempt writes from retry writes"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry distributed tracing in place","gaps":["Traces should tag retry attempts vs first attempts to make duplicate analysis possible"]},"debugging_capability":"Good for latency and throughput debugging. Poor for data correctness debugging — an on-call engineer at 3am cannot distinguish legitimate event volume from duplicate inflation without querying the warehouse directly."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Data warehouse transient failure triggers retries","handling":"partial","recommendation":"MUST add idempotency keys (e.g., event_id + SHA of payload) and upsert/INSERT IGNORE semantics in warehouse writes. Without this, the retry mechanism actively causes data corruption."},{"scenario":"ACK lost between warehouse and service","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Same fix — idempotent writes. The event_id must be generated at ingestion time and carried through the entire pipeline."},{"scenario":"Dead-letter queue fills up","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"MUST deploy a DLQ consumer that (1) alerts on queue depth > threshold, (2) retries with backoff to warehouse, (3) archives to cold storage after N failures. Without this, the DLQ is theater — it looks like a safety net but catches nothing."},{"scenario":"Sustained warehouse outage beyond retry budget","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker trips correctly, but events then flow to an unmonitored DLQ. Need alerting on circuit breaker state changes and DLQ inflow rate."},{"scenario":"Duplicate records corrupt downstream analytics","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a deduplication layer — either at write time (idempotent upserts) or as a warehouse-side scheduled job. Prefer write-time dedup to avoid delayed correctness."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff with 3 retries is well-configured mechanically, but actively harmful without idempotent writes — each retry doubles down on data corruption.","timeout_config":"Not explicitly mentioned but assumed configured given the load test results.","circuit_breakers":"Present on warehouse writes. Good pattern, but circuit breaker recovery (half-open state) will also produce duplicates when re-trying queued events.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Circuit breaker prevents cascade, but degraded-mode events go to a DLQ that nobody reads."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green deployment with load balancer traffic switch enables instant rollback. This is well-designed.","monitoring_coverage":"Strong for availability and latency. Blind spot on data correctness — no way to detect duplicate records or DLQ accumulation without manual investigation.","on_call_documentation":"Needs runbook entries for: (1) how to detect and remediate duplicate warehouse records, (2) how to drain and reprocess the DLQ, (3) what to do when circuit breaker is tripped.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/reprocessor service"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add idempotency key generation at ingestion layer","Configure warehouse writes as upserts or INSERT IGNORE on idempotency key"],"infra_provisioning":["DLQ depth monitoring and alerting","Dashboard panel for duplicate event rate"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Warehouse writes currently append without deduplication. Once duplicates are written, remediation requires a backfill job to deduplicate potentially billions of rows. The longer this ships without idempotent writes, the more expensive cleanup becomes. Adding a unique constraint or dedup key to a large clickstream table may also require a table rebuild depending on the warehouse engine."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent writes with at-least-once delivery guarantee","reason":"This is not an edge case — it is a mathematical certainty that duplicates will be produced under normal operation. The retry mechanism, circuit breaker recovery, and lost-ACK scenarios all independently produce duplicates. Shipping this corrupts analytics data from minute one. Fix: generate an idempotency key (event_id) at HTTP ingestion, carry it through the pipeline, and use upsert/INSERT IGNORE at the warehouse. This is a must-fix, not a nice-to-have.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer, no alerting, no reprocessing","reason":"A DLQ without a consumer is worse than no DLQ — it gives false confidence that failures are handled. In reality, every event that hits the DLQ is silently lost. Need: (1) a DLQ consumer service, (2) alerting on queue depth, (3) a reprocessing strategy with its own retry budget.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No metrics for data correctness or duplicate detection","reason":"The observability stack is excellent for availability but completely blind to the primary failure mode of this system: duplicate data. An on-call engineer cannot detect or quantify the problem without manually querying the warehouse. Need: duplicate rate metric, retry-vs-first-attempt tagging in traces, DLQ depth metric.","routed_to":"Shaq"}],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Idempotency gap is a hard blocker — duplicates are guaranteed, not theoretical","DLQ without consumer is a clear infrastructure gap","Observability for availability is genuinely strong"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact warehouse engine unknown — dedup strategy (upsert vs INSERT IGNORE vs merge) depends on this","Timeout configuration not explicitly provided, assumed from load test results"],"assumptions":["At-least-once delivery semantics are correctly described","Load test did not measure data correctness, only throughput and latency","No existing deduplication exists downstream in the warehouse or BI layer"]}}
